---
title: "SHTns"
subtitle: "Fast Spherical Harmonic Transforms"
---

[SHTns](https://nschaeff.bitbucket.io/shtns/) is a high-performance C library
for Spherical Harmonic Transforms — the forward and inverse transforms between
values on a sphere and their spherical-harmonic coefficients. It provides a
Fortran interface and is optimized with vectorized kernels.

**Version vendored:** 3.7.5 (`SHTns/shtns-3.7.5/`).

## Role in fesm-utils

SHTns supplies the spherical-harmonic machinery used by downstream models (e.g.
spectral transforms on the sphere). It **links against the [FFTW](fftw.qmd)
built in this repository**, so FFTW must be installed first.

## Building

SHTns is built by its own autotools through the root `Makefile`:

```bash
make fftw  openmp=0        # prerequisite: build FFTW first
make shtns openmp=0        # serial -> SHTns/shtns-serial
make shtns openmp=1        # OpenMP -> SHTns/shtns-omp
```

The target runs, in `SHTns/shtns-3.7.5/`, pointing SHTns at the FFTW prefix:

```bash
./configure --prefix=<repo>/SHTns/shtns-<variant> [--enable-openmp] \
    CPPFLAGS='-I<repo>/fftw/fftw-<variant>/include' \
    LDFLAGS='-L<repo>/fftw/fftw-<variant>/lib'
make && make install
```

The serial and OpenMP variants install side by side into `SHTns/shtns-serial`
and `SHTns/shtns-omp`. Machine/compiler specifics — the kernel-compiler choice
and `-march` handling — live in
[`config/libs.mk`](https://github.com/fesmc/fesm-utils/blob/main/config/libs.mk).

## Using it downstream

Reference the install prefix directly:

```
fesm-utils/SHTns/shtns-serial/include    # shtns.h, shtns.f03
fesm-utils/SHTns/shtns-serial/lib        # libshtns.a
```

## Notes

- SHTns is the last of the three external libraries to build, because of its
  FFTW link dependency. `make libs` builds `fftw`, `lis`, `shtns` in that order.
- Build the external libraries serially (do not pass `-j` to `make libs`).
